---
title: "Daniel Wang: Project Portfolio"
---

[Spatial Analysis Project](spatial_analysis.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener"}

# Demo

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

# Introduction

## About Me

Hello, I'm Daniel Wang and I recently graduated from with a masters degree in statistics from NC State. This is a portfolio I've made to showcase projects covering data cleaning, visualization, and statistical modelling. These were challenging experiences that highlight the methods and tools I use when working with data. My goal is to not only procure informative, valid analyses but also to clearly communicate the results to a broad audience.

Studying statistics has given me an opportunity to use mathematics, statistical methods, and critical thinking to tackle complex problems. Tools such as R, Python, or SAS are languages I regularly use for cleaning, modelling, and visualizing data among other purposes. Hopefully, you won't need my convincing and the projects shown here can do the talking!

## Education and Experience

# Projects

## Querying with Macros

## Prediction of Vaccine Severity Response

## Neuse Township: Traffic Crime Analysis

## Modeling March Madness Score Differentials
